title: Discourse Live — a real-time window on the world’s press
tagline: The world's press, live: every 15 minutes GDELT's news firehose becomes headlines you query in plain English

functionality.name: Live GDELT stream
functionality.blurb: Every 15 minutes it streams what the world's media just wrote into a fast analytics database, roughly 120,000+ articles a day.

functionality.name: Rich article tagging
functionality.blurb: Each article is tagged with themes, named entities, a six-axis emotional tone, and ~2,300 affect dimensions.

functionality.name: Plain-English querying
functionality.blurb: Ask a question in plain English and it writes a safe database query in front of you, returning fresh headlines within minutes of publication.

functionality.name: Planetary mood map
functionality.blurb: It paints the planet's mood as a clay-to-teal spectrum.

ops_note: The ClickHouse + Postgres stack is always-on, but the public gdelt.elua2.org address is a Cloudflare tunnel — on ps14 run gdelt-tunnel once per boot and keep the window open.
detail_body: The global news firehose, live. Every 15 minutes GDELT publishes what the world's media just wrote, and this app streams that into a fast analytics database — roughly 120,000+ articles a day. Each is tagged with themes, named entities, a six-axis emotional tone, and ~2,300 affect dimensions.
Ask a question in plain English and it writes a safe database query in front of you, returns fresh headlines within minutes of publication, and paints the planet's mood as a clay-to-teal spectrum.
